  "text": "Kafka's Java/Scala service initialization lifecycle is built around a small set of core abstractions:\n\n1. **`Server` trait** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/Server.scala`): The top-level lifecycle interface defining `startup()`, `shutdown()`, and `awaitShutdown()`. It also defines the `ProcessStatus` sealed trait with states SHUTDOWN, STARTING, STARTED, and SHUTTING_DOWN that servers use to guard against concurrent state transitions.\n\n2. **`KafkaBroker` trait** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaBroker.scala`): Extends the broker-specific lifecycle interface with `startup()`, `shutdown(timeout)`, `awaitShutdown()`, and `isShutdown()` along with accessor methods for all major broker subsystems.\n\n3. **`KafkaRaftServer`** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaRaftServer.scala`): The concrete `Server` implementation for KRaft mode. Implements coordinated startup ordering: **controller starts first** (so it can handle broker registration), then broker. For shutdown: **broker shuts down first** (controlled shutdown via the controller), then controller. This reverse-order shutdown ensures dependent services stop before their dependencies.\n\n4. **`BrokerServer`** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/BrokerServer.scala`): Implements `KafkaBroker`. Uses `maybeChangeStatus()` with the `ProcessStatus` enum to prevent concurrent startups or shutdowns. During shutdown, it performs a controlled shutdown by calling `replicaManager.beginControlledShutdown()` and `lifecycleManager.beginControlledShutdown()`, waiting for the controller to confirm partition leadership has been transferred before fully stopping.\n\n5. **`ControllerServer`** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ControllerServer.scala`): Implements the controller process lifecycle with the same `maybeChangeStatus()` pattern.\n\n6. **`BrokerLifecycleManager`** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/BrokerLifecycleManager.scala`): Owns the broker state machine (registered, fenced, active, shutting down). Manages broker registration with the controller (KIP-631), periodic heartbeats, and the controlled shutdown sequence. Exposes `beginShutdown()` and `beginControlledShutdown()` to coordinate graceful shutdown. Uses an event queue to process state transitions sequentially.\n\n7. **`SharedServer`** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/SharedServer.scala`): Manages components shared between BrokerServer and ControllerServer in combined mode (Raft manager, metadata loader, snapshot generator). Implements reference-counting start/stop via `startForBroker`/`startForController` and `stopForBroker`/`stopForController`, ensuring shared resources are only torn down when neither broker nor controller needs them.\n\n8. **`ShutdownableThread`** (`server-common/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/util/ShutdownableThread.java`): The foundational Java abstract class used throughout Kafka for worker threads. Provides `initiateShutdown()` (signals the thread to stop via a CountDownLatch and optional interrupt), `awaitShutdown()` (blocks until the thread completes), and `shutdown()` (combines both). Subclasses implement `doWork()` which is called in a loop until shutdown is initiated.\n\n9. **`Scheduler` interface / `KafkaScheduler`** (`server-common/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/util/Scheduler.java`, `KafkaScheduler.java`): Scheduler service with `startup()` and `shutdown()` lifecycle methods, used throughout the broker for periodic tasks.\n\n10. **`RaftManager`** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/raft/RaftManager.scala`): The Raft consensus layer with `startup()` and `shutdown()` lifecycle methods.\n\n11. **`Kafka` object** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/Kafka.scala`): The main entry point. Registers a JVM shutdown hook via `Exit.addShutdownHook` that calls `server.shutdown()` on SIGTERM/SIGINT, then calls `server.startup()` followed by `server.awaitShutdown()` to block the main thread until shutdown completes.\n\nThe coordinated shutdown sequence ensures dependent services stop in reverse startup order: broker stops first (performing a controlled shutdown to transfer partition leadership), then the controller stops, then the shared Raft/metadata infrastructure stops last."
}

# Java Service Initialization Lifecycle Pattern in Kafka

## Your Task

Find all Java source files in apache/kafka that implement the Startable/Stoppable service lifecycle: the Lifecycle interface (or equivalent), the service startup ordering, and the coordinated shutdown sequence that ensures dependent services stop in reverse order.

## Context

## Kafka Service Initialization Lifecycle Pattern

The lifecycle is implemented across these key files in `sg-evals/kafka--0753c489`:

### Core Interface
- **`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/Server.scala`** — The `Server` trait (Startable/Stoppable equivalent) defining `startup()`, `shutdown()`, `awaitShutdown()`, plus `ProcessStatus` states (SHUTDOWN, STARTING, STARTED, SHUTTING_DOWN)
- **`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaBroker.scala`** — Broker-specific lifecycle trait

### Implementations
- **`KafkaRaftServer`** — Orchestrates startup/shutdown ordering: **controller first, broker last** on startup; **broker first, controller last** on shutdown (reverse order)
- **`BrokerServer`** — Implements `KafkaBroker`, performs controlled shutdown (waits for partition leadership transfer)
- **`ControllerServer`** — Implements the controller process lifecycle
- **`BrokerLifecycleManager`** — State machine managing registration, heartbeats, and coordinated shutdown
- **`SharedServer`** — Shared Raft/metadata components with reference-counting start/stop

### Worker Thread Lifecycle
- **`ShutdownableThread`** (`server-common/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/util/ShutdownableThread.java`) — Abstract Java base class with `initiateShutdown()` + `awaitShutdown()` pattern used throughout the codebase

### Entry Point
- **`kafka.Kafka`** — Registers a JVM shutdown hook, calls `startup()`, blocks on `awaitShutdown()`

33: object Server {
34:   val MetricsPrefix: String = "kafka.server"
35:   val ClusterIdLabel: String = "kafka.cluster.id"
36:   val NodeIdLabel: String = "kafka.node.id"
37: 
38:   def initializeMetrics(
39:     config: KafkaConfig,
40:     time: Time,
41:     clusterId: String
42:   ): Metrics = {
43:     val metricsContext = createKafkaMetricsContext(config, clusterId)
44:     buildMetrics(config, time, metricsContext)
45:   }
46: 
47:   private def buildMetrics(
48:     config: KafkaConfig,
49:     time: Time,
50:     metricsContext: KafkaMetricsContext
51:   ): Metrics = {
52:     val metricConfig = buildMetricsConfig(config)
53:     new Metrics(metricConfig, new util.ArrayList[MetricsReporter](), time, true, metricsContext)
54:   }
55: 
56:   def buildMetricsConfig(
57:     kafkaConfig: KafkaConfig
58:   ): MetricConfig = {
59:     new MetricConfig()
60:       .samples(kafkaConfig.metricNumSamples)
61:       .recordLevel(Sensor.RecordingLevel.forName(kafkaConfig.metricRecordingLevel))
62:       .timeWindow(kafkaConfig.metricSampleWindowMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)
63:   }
64: 
65:   private[server] def createKafkaMetricsContext(
66:     config: KafkaConfig,
67:     clusterId: String
68:   ): KafkaMetricsContext = {
69:     val contextLabels = new java.util.HashMap[String, Object]
70:     contextLabels.put(ClusterIdLabel, clusterId)
71:     contextLabels.put(NodeIdLabel, config.nodeId.toString)
72:     contextLabels.putAll(config.originalsWithPrefix(CommonClientConfigs.METRICS_CONTEXT_PREFIX))
73:     new KafkaMetricsContext(MetricsPrefix, contextLabels)
74:   }
75: 
76:   sealed trait ProcessStatus
77:   case object SHUTDOWN extends ProcessStatus
78:   case object STARTING extends ProcessStatus
79:   case object STARTED extends ProcessStatus
80:   case object SHUTTING_DOWN extends ProcessStatus
81: }

40: /**
41:  * This class implements the KRaft (Kafka Raft) mode server which relies
42:  * on a KRaft quorum for maintaining cluster metadata. It is responsible for
43:  * constructing the controller and/or broker based on the `process.roles`
44:  * configuration and for managing their basic lifecycle (startup and shutdown).
45:  *
46:  */
47: class KafkaRaftServer(
48:   config: KafkaConfig,
49:   time: Time,
50: ) extends Server with Logging {
51: 
52:   this.logIdent = s"[KafkaRaftServer nodeId=${config.nodeId}] "
53: 
54:   private val (metaPropsEnsemble, bootstrapMetadata) =
55:     KafkaRaftServer.initializeLogDirs(config, this.logger.underlying, this.logIdent)
56: 
57:   private val metrics = Server.initializeMetrics(
58:     config,
59:     time,
60:     metaPropsEnsemble.clusterId().get()
61:   )
62: 
63:   private val sharedServer = new SharedServer(
64:     config,
65:     metaPropsEnsemble,
66:     time,
67:     metrics,
68:     CompletableFuture.completedFuture(QuorumConfig.parseVoterConnections(config.quorumConfig.voters)),
69:     QuorumConfig.parseBootstrapServers(config.quorumConfig.bootstrapServers),
70:     new StandardFaultHandlerFactory(),
71:     ServerSocketFactory.INSTANCE,
72:   )
73: 
74:   private val broker: Option[BrokerServer] = if (config.processRoles.contains(ProcessRole.BrokerRole)) {
75:     Some(new BrokerServer(sharedServer))
76:   } else {
77:     None
78:   }
79: 
80:   private val controller: Option[ControllerServer] = if (config.processRoles.contains(ProcessRole.ControllerRole)) {
81:     Some(new ControllerServer(
82:       sharedServer,
83:       KafkaRaftServer.configSchema,
84:       bootstrapMetadata,
85:     ))
86:   } else {
87:     None
88:   }
89: 
90:   override def startup(): Unit = {
91:     Mx4jLoader.maybeLoad()
92:     // Controller component must be started before the broker component so that
93:     // the controller endpoints are passed to the KRaft manager
94:     controller.foreach(_.startup())
95:     broker.foreach(_.startup())
96:     AppInfoParser.registerAppInfo(Server.MetricsPrefix, config.brokerId.toString, metrics, time.milliseconds())
97:     info(KafkaBroker.STARTED_MESSAGE)
98:   }
99: 
100:   override def shutdown(): Unit = {
101:     // In combined mode, we want to shut down the broker first, since the controller may be
102:     // needed for controlled shutdown. Additionally, the controller shutdown process currently
103:     // stops the raft client early on, which would disrupt broker shutdown.
104:     broker.foreach(_.shutdown())
105:     controller.foreach(_.shutdown())
106:     CoreUtils.swallow(AppInfoParser.unregisterAppInfo(Server.MetricsPrefix, config.brokerId.toString, metrics), this)
107:   }
108: 
109:   override def awaitShutdown(): Unit = {
110:     broker.foreach(_.awaitShutdown())
111:     controller.foreach(_.awaitShutdown())
112:   }
113: }
114: 
115: object KafkaRaftServer {
116:   val MetadataTopic = Topic.CLUSTER_METADATA_TOPIC_NAME
117:   val MetadataPartition = Topic.CLUSTER_METADATA_TOPIC_PARTITION
118:   val MetadataTopicId = Uuid.METADATA_TOPIC_ID
119: 
120:   /**
121:    * Initialize the configured log directories, including both [[KRaftConfigs.MetadataLogDirProp]]
122:    * and [[KafkaConfig.LOG_DIR_PROP]]. This method performs basic validation to ensure that all
123:    * directories are accessible and have been initialized with consistent `meta.properties`.
124:    *
125:    * @param config The process configuration
126:    * @return A tuple containing the loaded meta properties (which are guaranteed to
127:    *         be consistent across all log dirs) and the offline directories
128:    */
129:   def initializeLogDirs(
130:     config: KafkaConfig,
131:     log: Logger,
132:     logPrefix: String
133:   ): (MetaPropertiesEnsemble, BootstrapMetadata) = {
134:     // Load and verify the original ensemble.
135:     val loader = new MetaPropertiesEnsemble.Loader()
136:     loader.addMetadataLogDir(config.metadataLogDir)
137:           .addLogDirs(config.logDirs)
138:     val initialMetaPropsEnsemble = loader.load()
139:     val verificationFlags = util.EnumSet.of(REQUIRE_AT_LEAST_ONE_VALID, REQUIRE_METADATA_LOG_DIR)
140:     initialMetaPropsEnsemble.verify(Optional.empty(), OptionalInt.of(config.nodeId), verificationFlags)
141: 
142:     // Check that the __cluster_metadata-0 topic does not appear outside the metadata directory.
143:     val metadataPartitionDirName = UnifiedLog.logDirName(MetadataPartition)
144:     initialMetaPropsEnsemble.logDirProps().keySet().forEach(logDir => {
145:       if (!logDir.equals(config.metadataLogDir)) {
146:         val clusterMetadataTopic = new File(logDir, metadataPartitionDirName)
147:         if (clusterMetadataTopic.exists) {
148:           throw new KafkaException(s"Found unexpected metadata location in data directory `$clusterMetadataTopic` " +
149:             s"(the configured metadata directory is ${config.metadataLogDir}).")
150:         }
151:       }
152:     })
153: 
154:     // Set directory IDs on all directories. Rewrite the files if needed.
155:     val metaPropsEnsemble = {
156:       val copier = new MetaPropertiesEnsemble.Copier(initialMetaPropsEnsemble)
157:       initialMetaPropsEnsemble.nonFailedDirectoryProps().forEachRemaining(e => {
158:         val logDir = e.getKey
159:         val metaProps = e.getValue
160:         if (!metaProps.isPresent()) {
161:           throw new RuntimeException(s"No `meta.properties` found in $logDir (have you run `kafka-storage.sh` " +
162:             "to format the directory?)")
163:         }
164:         if (!metaProps.get().nodeId().isPresent()) {
165:           throw new RuntimeException(s"Error: node ID not found in $logDir")
166:         }
167:         if (!metaProps.get().clusterId().isPresent()) {
168:           throw new RuntimeException(s"Error: cluster ID not found in $logDir")
169:         }
170:         val builder = new MetaProperties.Builder(metaProps.get())
171:         if (!builder.directoryId().isPresent()) {
172:           builder.setDirectoryId(copier.generateValidDirectoryId())
173:         }
174:         copier.setLogDirProps(logDir, builder.build())
175:         copier.setPreWriteHandler((logDir, _, _) => {
176:           log.info("{}Rewriting {}{}meta.properties", logPrefix, logDir, File.separator)
177:         })
178:       })
179:       copier.writeLogDirChanges()
180:       copier.copy()
181:     }
182: 
183:     // Load the BootstrapMetadata.
184:     val bootstrapDirectory = new BootstrapDirectory(config.metadataLogDir)
185:     val bootstrapMetadata = bootstrapDirectory.read()
186:     (metaPropsEnsemble, bootstrapMetadata)
187:   }
188: 
189:   val configSchema = new KafkaConfigSchema(Map(
190:     ConfigResource.Type.BROKER -> new ConfigDef(KafkaConfig.configDef),
191:     ConfigResource.Type.TOPIC -> LogConfig.configDefCopy,
192:   ).asJava, ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.ALL_TOPIC_CONFIG_SYNONYMS)
193: }

39: /**
40:  * The broker lifecycle manager owns the broker state.
41:  *
42:  * Its inputs are messages passed in from other parts of the broker and from the
43:  * controller: requests to start up, or shut down, for example. Its output are the broker
44:  * state and various futures that can be used to wait for broker state transitions to
45:  * occur.
46:  *
47:  * The lifecycle manager handles registering the broker with the controller, as described
48:  * in KIP-631. After registration is complete, it handles sending periodic broker
49:  * heartbeats and processing the responses.
50:  *
51:  * This code uses an event queue paradigm. Modifications get translated into events, which
52:  * are placed on the queue to be processed sequentially. As described in the JavaDoc for
53:  * each variable, most mutable state can be accessed only from that event queue thread.
54:  * In some cases we expose a volatile variable which can be read from any thread, but only
55:  * written from the event queue thread.
56:  */
57: class BrokerLifecycleManager(
58:   val config: KafkaConfig,
59:   val time: Time,
60:   val threadNamePrefix: String,
61:   val logDirs: Set[Uuid],
62:   val shutdownHook: () => Unit = () => {}
63: ) extends Logging {
64: 
65:   private def logPrefix(): String = {
66:     val builder = new StringBuilder("[BrokerLifecycleManager")
67:     builder.append(" id=").append(config.nodeId)
68:     builder.append("] ")
69:     builder.toString()
70:   }
71: 
72:   val logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix())
73: 
74:   this.logIdent = logContext.logPrefix()
75: 
76:   /**
77:    * The broker id.
78:    */
79:   private val nodeId = config.nodeId
80: 
81:   /**
82:    * The broker rack, or null if there is no configured rack.
83:    */
84:   private val rack = config.rack
85: 
86:   /**
87:    * How long to wait for registration to succeed before failing the startup process.
88:    */
89:   private val initialTimeoutNs =
90:     MILLISECONDS.toNanos(config.initialRegistrationTimeoutMs.longValue())
91: 
92:   /**
93:    * The broker incarnation ID.  This ID uniquely identifies each time we start the broker
94:    */
95:   val incarnationId: Uuid = Uuid.randomUuid()

18: package kafka
19: 
20: import java.util.Properties
21: import joptsimple.OptionParser
22: import kafka.server.{KafkaConfig, KafkaRaftServer, Server}
23: import kafka.utils.Implicits._
24: import kafka.utils.Logging
25: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.{Exit, Java, LoggingSignalHandler, OperatingSystem, Time, Utils}
26: import org.apache.kafka.server.util.CommandLineUtils
27: 
28: object Kafka extends Logging {
29: 
30:   def getPropsFromArgs(args: Array[String]): Properties = {
31:     val optionParser = new OptionParser(false)
32:     val overrideOpt = optionParser.accepts("override", "Optional property that should override values set in server.properties file")
33:       .withRequiredArg()
34:       .ofType(classOf[String])
35:     // This is just to make the parameter show up in the help output, we are not actually using this due the
36:     // fact that this class ignores the first parameter which is interpreted as positional and mandatory
37:     // but would not be mandatory if --version is specified
38:     // This is a bit of an ugly crutch till we get a chance to rework the entire command line parsing
39:     optionParser.accepts("version", "Print version information and exit.")
40: 
41:     if (args.isEmpty || args.contains("--help")) {
42:       CommandLineUtils.printUsageAndExit(optionParser,
43:         "USAGE: java [options] %s server.properties [--override property=value]*".format(this.getClass.getCanonicalName.split('$').head))
44:     }
45: 
46:     if (args.contains("--version")) {
47:       CommandLineUtils.printVersionAndExit()
48:     }
49: 
50:     val props = Utils.loadProps(args(0))
51: 
52:     if (args.length > 1) {
53:       val options = optionParser.parse(args.slice(1, args.length): _*)
54: 
55:       if (options.nonOptionArguments().size() > 0) {
56:         CommandLineUtils.printUsageAndExit(optionParser, "Found non argument parameters: " + options.nonOptionArguments().toArray.mkString(","))
57:       }
58: 
59:       props ++= CommandLineUtils.parseKeyValueArgs(options.valuesOf(overrideOpt))
60:     }
61:     props
62:   }
63: 
64:   private def buildServer(props: Properties): Server = {
65:     val config = KafkaConfig.fromProps(props, doLog = false)
66:     new KafkaRaftServer(
67:       config,
68:       Time.SYSTEM,
69:     )
70:   }
71: 
72:   def main(args: Array[String]): Unit = {
73:     try {
74:       val serverProps = getPropsFromArgs(args)
75:       val server = buildServer(serverProps)
76: 
77:       try {
78:         if (!OperatingSystem.IS_WINDOWS && !Java.isIbmJdk)
79:           new LoggingSignalHandler().register()
80:       } catch {
81:         case e: ReflectiveOperationException =>
82:           warn("Failed to register optional signal handler that logs a message when the process is terminated " +
83:             s"by a signal. Reason for registration failure is: $e", e)
84:       }
85: 
86:       // attach shutdown handler to catch terminating signals as well as normal termination
87:       Exit.addShutdownHook("kafka-shutdown-hook", () => {
88:         try server.shutdown()
89:         catch {
90:           case _: Throwable =>
91:             fatal("Halting Kafka.")
92:             // Calling exit() can lead to deadlock as exit() can be called multiple times. Force exit.
93:             Exit.halt(1)
94:         }
95:       })
96: 
97:       try server.startup()
98:       catch {
99:         case e: Throwable =>
100:           // KafkaBroker.startup() calls shutdown() in case of exceptions, so we invoke `exit` to set the status code
101:           fatal("Exiting Kafka due to fatal exception during startup.", e)
102:           Exit.exit(1)
103:       }
104: 
105:       server.awaitShutdown()
106:     }
107:     catch {
108:       case e: Throwable =>
109:         fatal("Exiting Kafka due to fatal exception", e)
110:         Exit.exit(1)
111:     }
112:     Exit.exit(0)
113:   }
114: }

82:  * The SharedServer manages the components which are shared between the BrokerServer and
83:  * ControllerServer. These shared components include the Raft manager, snapshot generator,
84:  * and metadata loader. A KRaft server running in combined mode as both a broker and a controller
85:  * will still contain only a single SharedServer instance.
86:  *
87:  * The SharedServer will be started as soon as either the broker or the controller needs it,
88:  * via the appropriate function (startForBroker or startForController). Similarly, it will be
89:  * stopped as soon as neither the broker nor the controller need it, via stopForBroker or
90:  * stopForController. One way of thinking about this is that both the broker and the controller
91:  * could hold a "reference" to this class, and we don't truly stop it until both have dropped
92:  * their reference. We opted to use two booleans here rather than a reference count in order to
93:  * make debugging easier and reduce the chance of resource leaks.

  "text": "Kafka's Java/Scala service initialization lifecycle is built around a small set of core abstractions:\n\n1. **`Server` trait** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/Server.scala`): The top-level lifecycle interface defining `startup()`, `shutdown()`, and `awaitShutdown()`. It also defines the `ProcessStatus` sealed trait with states SHUTDOWN, STARTING, STARTED, and SHUTTING_DOWN that servers use to guard against concurrent state transitions.\n\n2. **`KafkaBroker` trait** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaBroker.scala`): Extends the broker-specific lifecycle interface with `startup()`, `shutdown(timeout)`, `awaitShutdown()`, and `isShutdown()` along with accessor methods for all major broker subsystems.\n\n3. **`KafkaRaftServer`** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaRaftServer.scala`): The concrete `Server` implementation for KRaft mode. Implements coordinated startup ordering: **controller starts first** (so it can handle broker registration), then broker. For shutdown: **broker shuts down first** (controlled shutdown via the controller), then controller. This reverse-order shutdown ensures dependent services stop before their dependencies.\n\n4. **`BrokerServer`** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/BrokerServer.scala`): Implements `KafkaBroker`. Uses `maybeChangeStatus()` with the `ProcessStatus` enum to prevent concurrent startups or shutdowns. During shutdown, it performs a controlled shutdown by calling `replicaManager.beginControlledShutdown()` and `lifecycleManager.beginControlledShutdown()`, waiting for the controller to confirm partition leadership has been transferred before fully stopping.\n\n5. **`ControllerServer`** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ControllerServer.scala`): Implements the controller process lifecycle with the same `maybeChangeStatus()` pattern.\n\n6. **`BrokerLifecycleManager`** (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/BrokerLifecycleManager.scala`): Owns the broker state machine (registered, fenced, active, shutting down). Manages broker registration with the controller (KIP-631), periodic heartbeats, and the controlled shutdown sequence.
